body.title: [sprint] Stamp run_id on every io_corpus record so a run's payloads are a query, not a grep
body.description:
Increment 2 of T-inference-run-record. The run ticket names each hop but nothing links a hop to the io_corpus records that carry its bytes, so 'show me the payloads for this run' is still a hand-rolled grep — the exact friction the run record exists to remove.

WHY NOT AN io_ref PER HOP: one hop is NOT one dispatch. GeneralDomain does one; CodingDomain's architect/editor split does several. A single io_ref per hop is wrong by construction, and threading a LIST up through LoopResult/agentic_loop is invasive.

SHAPE: add `run_id: str = ''` to InferenceRequest — a sibling of `ticket_id`, which already threads through to the io record — set it in BaseDomain.run, and capture it in io_corpus. Then 'payloads for this run' is `run_id`, and 'payloads for hop N' is `run_id + escalation_hop` (io records already carry escalation_hop). No list, no LoopResult change.

NOT CONTEXTVARS. A contextvar-propagated correlator is invisible on the object you would inspect — precisely the 'the data was there but didn't show up when I looked' failure this whole line of work exists to fix. Also they do not cross thread boundaries, and dispatch may run threaded (T-inference-single-device-instance-no-thread-per-call).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/shim.py (InferenceRequest), device.py (capture block), io_corpus.py, domains/base.py, domains/general.py, domains/agentic_loop.py, domains/architect_editor.py
**Design rules:** log every interface crossing; homogeneity over special-case (device.py's internal self-test/announce requests legitimately get run_id='')
**Scope boundary:** IN — the run_id field, its threading through the domain-path request constructors, its capture, and a reader that resolves a run to its payloads. OUT — changing what io_corpus captures per call (T-io-corpus-text-drops-reasoning).
**Test plan:** hermetic unit, red->green, NO device. Build an InferenceRequest(run_id='R'), pass it through the request->io-record mapping, assert the record carries 'R'. THE DISCRIMINATOR: a second test asserts that a request with run_id='' still produces a valid record (device-internal calls must not be broken by the new field), and that two hops of one run share a run_id while carrying different escalation_hop values — a stamp that collapsed both hops into one would pass a naive single-hop test.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
